scenarios.title: Valid local portfolio import

gherkin.given: the import contains recognized holding fields and no secret or account-identity field
gherkin.when: the user reviews and confirms the import preview
gherkin.then:
one new local portfolio revision becomes current
And holdings, quantities, optional cost fields, and derived values remain local-only
And the Portfolio Brief and portfolio analyses reference the new revision

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-foundation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-001 valid local portfolio import creates one current revision

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-001 valid local portfolio import creates one current revision

expectedAssertions: preview identifies accepted normalized duplicate unresolved and rejected rows
confirm and reload expose exactly one new current revision
request and public-state ledgers contain no personal import field

scenarios.title: Invalid import is atomic

gherkin.given:
a current valid portfolio exists
And a new import contains malformed rows, credential-shaped fields, or unresolved required identities
gherkin.then:
the requested revision is rejected with row and field reasons
And the prior portfolio remains current and unchanged
And no rejected value enters storage, logs, URLs, telemetry, or committed artifacts

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-foundation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-002 invalid or secret-bearing import is atomic and redacted

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-002 invalid or secret-bearing import is atomic and redacted

expectedAssertions: confirmation remains disabled and errors expose only safe row field and P008 code
the prior portfolio and active hashes remain unchanged
the secret sentinel is absent from storage request console URL and public artifact inventories

scenarios.title: Explicit mandate owns hard constraints

gherkin.given: the user explicitly enters a horizon, a dated cash need, and allocation bounds
gherkin.then:
every affected result cites those user-entered constraints
And behavior history does not add, remove, or modify a constraint
And missing mandate fields remain absent rather than inferred

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-foundation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-003 explicit mandate alone supplies every hard constraint

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-003 explicit mandate alone supplies every hard constraint

expectedAssertions: Path and Allocation rows cite the same MandateRevision identity
behavior events do not alter bound date amount objective or expected-return inputs
missing mandate fields remain visibly absent

scenarios.title: No mandate means no goal-fit claim

gherkin.given:
a valid local portfolio exists
But no valid mandate or cash need exists
gherkin.when: the user opens Risk X-Ray, Path Lab, or Allocation Comparison
gherkin.then:
descriptive distributions and comparisons may be shown
And goal-fit and survival-to-goal states are unavailable with an explicit reason
And no default goal, risk tolerance, or liquidity need is invented

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-foundation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-004 no mandate leaves goal fit and survival unavailable

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-004 no mandate leaves goal fit and survival unavailable

expectedAssertions: descriptive risk path and candidate rows remain available where data permits
goal-fit and survival state are unavailable with an exact reason
no floor tolerance horizon liquidity need or risk profile appears

scenarios.title: Generic publisher never receives personal context

gherkin.given: the browser holds quantities, cost basis, P&L, cash needs, and behavior events
gherkin.when: the public four-window publisher refreshes generic evidence
gherkin.then:
none of those local fields is included in a publisher input or output
And committed payload, snapshot, and history remain portfolio-agnostic
And personalized composition occurs only after generic evidence reaches the local context

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-foundation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-005 generic publisher and public requests contain no personal sentinel

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-005 generic publisher and public requests contain no personal sentinel

expectedAssertions: publisher argv environment inputs outputs and public files contain no personal sentinel
requests URLs referrers console and RLDATA public read contain no sentinel
local actions retain a private workspace fingerprint without public writeback

scenarios.title: Four-window local composition

gherkin.given:
a fresh generic pre-market morning pre-close or after-hours evidence record exists
And a valid local portfolio or direct research scope exists
gherkin.when: the user opens the Portfolio Brief for that window
gherkin.then:
the brief identifies the exact window and its evidence cutoff
And portfolio-specific research actions use only evidence available by that cutoff
And local composition time remains distinct from generic publication time

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-brief.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-006 all four exact ET windows preserve cutoff and composition time

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-006 all four exact ET windows preserve cutoff and composition time

expectedAssertions: all four exact IDs and ET labels render
generic cutoff publication and local composition times remain separate
an observation after the selected cutoff is absent and an unavailable slot is not substituted

scenarios.title: Direct and inferred scope stay separate

gherkin.given:
one ticker is in local holdings
And one ticker is only in the public watchlist
And one ticker appears only in eligible explicitly completed research action history
And one domain appears only through relevance inferred from eligible explicitly completed research action history
gherkin.then:
the held ticker, watchlist ticker, completed-research ticker, and inferred domain appear in the Held, Public watchlist, Completed research, and Inferred relevance lanes respectively
And each item states the correct direct or behavior-derived scope source
And no inferred ticker is presented as held
And no held ticker is presented as proof of user interest or risk preference

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-brief.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-007 held watch completed-research and inferred-relevance lanes reject raw history

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-007 held watch completed-research and inferred-relevance lanes reject raw history

expectedAssertions: held public-watchlist completed-research and inferred-relevance lanes are distinct
every action repeats its exact source authority
raw view open and click history contributes to neither completed-research nor inferred-relevance scope
no inferred row contains ownership or mandate fields and no held row claims preference

scenarios.title: Every behavior-derived item explains why it appears

gherkin.given: eligible completed actions support a non-sensitive domain interest
gherkin.when: a general research action is ranked from that interest
gherkin.then:
it shows why shown event categories relevance confidence horizon recency decay freshness trigger and completion or invalidation condition
And it links to the owning research surface
And it is labeled as a relevance inference rather than a personal constraint or market fact

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-brief.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-008 inferred action exposes complete why shown provenance

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-008 inferred action exposes complete why shown provenance

expectedAssertions: all required supporting categories confidence horizon recency decay freshness trigger and condition fields are visible
relevance confidence is separate from market or model confidence
owner route is fixed and the action is labeled inferred relevance not fact constraint or holding

scenarios.title: Settings never become inferred interests

gherkin.given: the user has no eligible behavior history for the affected domain
gherkin.then:
no InterestSignal is created or strengthened from those fields
And no risk tolerance loss capacity goal tax status or preferred asset is inferred
And calculation changes remain attributable to explicit user input only

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-brief.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-009 settings parameters scroll and dwell create no interest signal

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-009 settings parameters scroll and dwell create no interest signal

expectedAssertions: behavior event and interest arrays remain byte-identical
general-interest action identities remain unchanged
no trait preference goal tax or risk-profile field or copy appears

scenarios.title: Insufficient behavior history degrades honestly

gherkin.given: no eligible events survive the visible evidence and decay policy
gherkin.then:
behavior-derived interests are labeled insufficient
And the brief uses holdings public watchlist current context and generic evidence only
And no fabricated preference persona or general-interest recommendation appears

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-brief.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-010 insufficient completed history produces zero inferred actions

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-010 insufficient completed history produces zero inferred actions

expectedAssertions: the general-interest lane states insufficient completed research history
zero InterestSignal and inferred action rows exist
direct holdings watchlist and generic rows remain available without filler

scenarios.title: Clear behavior history removes its influence

gherkin.given: behavior-derived items currently affect brief ranking
gherkin.when: the user confirms Clear behavior history
gherkin.then:
eligible events and derived InterestSignals are removed locally
And the next composition contains no behavior-derived ranking influence
And holdings mandate cash needs and public watchlist remain unless separately cleared

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-foundation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-011 clear behavior removes ranking influence and preserves portfolio

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-011 clear behavior removes ranking influence and preserves portfolio

expectedAssertions: events interests and action outcomes verify empty in the new generation
the recomposed brief contains zero behavior-derived rank contribution
portfolio mandate cash-need generic-cache and watchlist hashes remain unchanged

scenarios.title: No engagement or sensitive profiling

gherkin.given: pointer movement dwell time scroll depth settings and sensitive-trait fields exist or can be observed
gherkin.then:
those sources are excluded
And only named completed research-action categories may contribute
And no cross-device identifier or hidden profile is created
And ranking optimizes research relevance rather than engagement

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-foundation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-012 behavior evidence excludes engagement and sensitive profiling

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-012 behavior evidence excludes engagement and sensitive profiling

expectedAssertions: only closed minimal completed-research fields persist
excluded click dwell scroll settings parameters raw text and sensitive-trait values are absent
no cross-device ID hidden score streak reward or engagement objective exists

scenarios.title: Arithmetic and geometric return remain distinct

gherkin.given: an aligned return sample and portfolio weights are valid
gherkin.then:
arithmetic mean compounded CAGR and observed volatility drag are shown separately
And the approximation g ~= mu - sigma squared over two is labeled conditional with assumptions
And no conclusion states that lower volatility universally produces higher wealth

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-risk.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-013 arithmetic CAGR and conditional drag stay separate

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-013 arithmetic CAGR and conditional drag stay separate

expectedAssertions: independently calculated arithmetic CAGR and drag values appear in separate rows
sample annualization and conditional approximation assumptions are visible
no lower-volatility winner or universal wealth conclusion exists

scenarios.title: Drawdown and unrecovered state are truthful

gherkin.given:
the sample includes a peak and subsequent drawdown
But no later observation regains that peak by the evidence cutoff
gherkin.then:
maximum drawdown peak date trough date and current depth are visible
And recovery is labeled unrecovered as of the cutoff
And no future recovery duration is fabricated

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-risk.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-014 unrecovered drawdown stops at the evidence cutoff

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-014 unrecovered drawdown stops at the evidence cutoff

expectedAssertions: peak trough maximum and current depth match the eligible sample
recovery state reads unrecovered as of the exact cutoff
a later excluded recovery and any fabricated duration are absent

scenarios.title: Concentration exposes overlapping risk

gherkin.given: compatible exposure data is available for holdings sharing issuer sector factor or constituents
gherkin.then:
position issuer sector factor and known look-through overlap remain separate views
And missing holdings detail is visible
And a concentration threshold is labeled as a research parameter rather than universal suitability

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-risk.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-015 concentration lenses expose overlap and missing look through

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-015 concentration lenses expose overlap and missing look through

expectedAssertions: position issuer sector factor and look-through render as separate lenses
each lens exposes covered count weight source date and missing detail
the concentration marker is labeled calibration and not suitability

scenarios.title: Beta R-squared and residual risk stay separate

gherkin.given: a named benchmark and aligned return window are valid for a portfolio with moderate beta and low explanatory power
gherkin.then:
beta R-squared alpha estimate and residual risk are reported separately
And the benchmark sample frequency and uncertainty are visible
And moderate beta is not described as low total risk

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-risk.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-016 beta alpha R squared and residual risk stay separate

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-016 beta alpha R squared and residual risk stay separate

expectedAssertions: beta alpha R-squared correlation and residual risk occupy separate labeled fields
benchmark sample frequency uncertainty and low-fit limitation are visible
moderate beta is never described as low total risk

scenarios.title: Marginal and total risk contribution reconcile

gherkin.given: finite weights and a valid covariance estimate exist
gherkin.when: asset and factor contributions are calculated
gherkin.then:
marginal and total contribution definitions are visible
And total contributions reconcile to portfolio risk within declared numerical tolerance
And unstable or non-positive-definite estimates are not silently repaired without disclosure

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-risk.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-017 marginal and total risk contributions reconcile

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-017 marginal and total risk contributions reconcile

expectedAssertions: MRC RC portfolio volatility sum and configured tolerance are visible
the contribution sum reconciles on the positive-definite fixture
singular raw and explicitly conditioned states remain separate with no silent lambda change

scenarios.title: Block-bootstrap paths are reproducible

gherkin.given: portfolio revision return sample block policy horizon cash flows fees and seed are identical
gherkin.then:
path identities and result summaries are identical
And block length and sampling assumptions are visible
And changing the seed or block policy creates a distinct ScenarioSpecification

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-paths.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-018 identical stationary bootstrap specification reproduces paths

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-018 identical stationary bootstrap specification reproduces paths

expectedAssertions: two identical runs expose equal index path and summary hashes
seed block policy source sample horizon flows and fees are visible
a changed seed or block length yields a distinct scenario identity

scenarios.title: Parameter uncertainty is part of survival

gherkin.given: the user selects explicit expected-return dependence or tail uncertainty ranges or an evidence-derived parameter policy
gherkin.then:
results show a distribution across parameter uncertainty as well as path randomness
And the most influential assumptions are identified
And one point estimate is not presented as the survival truth

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-paths.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-019 parameter uncertainty is separate from path randomness

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-019 parameter uncertainty is separate from path randomness

expectedAssertions: central path-randomness and across-parameter bands are separately labeled
combined distribution and assumption influence rows are visible
no single point estimate is labeled the survival truth

scenarios.title: Withdrawal collision changes the path outcome

gherkin.given:
the user entered the need amount and date explicitly
And a generated path falls before that date
gherkin.when: the withdrawal is applied at the declared time
gherkin.then:
the path records the cash-need collision and post-withdrawal capital
And survival or floor outcomes reflect sequence risk
And the need is not shifted or reduced to improve the result

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-paths.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-020 dated cash need records before and after collision capital

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-020 dated cash need records before and after collision capital

expectedAssertions: the need applies on the first modeled date on or after its declared date
capital before amount after funded fraction and downstream floor effect are visible
the declared date and amount remain unchanged

scenarios.title: Missing survival definition does not create a default

gherkin.given:
return history and portfolio weights are available
But survival success conditions are absent
gherkin.then:
wealth drawdown and cash-flow distributions may be shown
And survival probability is unavailable with a reason
And no hidden wealth floor withdrawal rate or success threshold is supplied

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-paths.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-021 missing survival definition renders distributions without probability

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-021 missing survival definition renders distributions without probability

expectedAssertions: wealth drawdown and cash-flow distributions remain available
survival state is unavailable with an exact missing-input reason
no floor withdrawal rate success threshold or probability appears

scenarios.title: Raw crisis correlation receives volatility context

gherkin.given: normal and stress samples are explicitly defined for a high-volatility stress period
gherkin.then:
raw correlations and volatility changes are shown
And an eligible volatility-adjusted estimate or an explicit unavailable reason is shown
And the product does not automatically label the raw increase contagion

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-diversification.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-022 raw stress correlation shows volatility context and qualified adjustment

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-022 raw stress correlation shows volatility context and qualified adjustment

expectedAssertions: raw normal/stress correlations and both variance states are visible
anchor orientation assumptions and adjusted estimate or exact unavailable reason are visible
no automatic contagion label exists

scenarios.title: Crisis correlation never becomes a universal one

gherkin.given: a finite stressed sample and uncertainty estimate exist for holdings with increased downside dependence
gherkin.then:
increased dependence and tail co-movement are visible
And thin-sample uncertainty is visible
And the read does not state that all assets always become perfectly correlated

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-diversification.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-023 finite tail evidence never claims universal correlation one

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-023 finite tail evidence never claims universal correlation one

expectedAssertions: tail estimate quantile joint-event count sample and interval are visible
below-floor evidence is explicitly unavailable
no all-assets always-perfectly-correlated statement exists

scenarios.title: Alternative appraisal smoothing limits decorrelation claims

gherkin.given: valuations for a manually valued real estate or collectible series are infrequent stale appraisal-based or user-entered
gherkin.when: normal correlation and volatility are shown
gherkin.then:
valuation frequency last appraisal liquidity expected transaction cost and smoothing caveat are prominent
And the asset is not treated as mechanically orthogonal
And sensitivity to a de-smoothed or stress assumption is required before a diversification conclusion

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-diversification.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-024 appraisal smoothing and illiquidity block mechanical decorrelation

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-024 appraisal smoothing and illiquidity block mechanical decorrelation

expectedAssertions: valuation age frequency method liquidity and transaction-cost state precede the result
observed and de-smoothed sensitivity remain separate
no mechanical orthogonality or low-risk conclusion is available

scenarios.title: Hedged and unhedged comparison includes carry and basis risk

gherkin.given: hedge proxy horizon carry transaction cost rebalance and basis-risk assumptions are explicit
gherkin.when: the hedged and unhedged comparison runs
gherkin.then:
gross risk change carry direct cost turnover residual exposure and net modeled outcome are separate
And missing cost evidence makes net benefit unavailable
And no hedge ratio is prescribed as personally optimal

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-diversification.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-025 hedge comparison separates gross carry costs basis and residual

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-025 hedge comparison separates gross carry costs basis and residual

expectedAssertions: gross carry direct turnover basis residual liquidity and net fields remain separate
net is unavailable when carry or cost evidence is missing
no optimal suitable prescribed or executable hedge ratio exists

scenarios.title: All six allocation methods share one comparison basis

gherkin.given: one frozen portfolio universe evidence set mandate and cost policy are valid
gherkin.when: current equal-weight minimum-variance risk-parity Black-Litterman and constrained MVO candidates run
gherkin.then:
every candidate uses the same shared inputs and constraints where applicable
And method-specific assumptions remain visible
And all feasible and infeasible candidates appear side by side

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-allocation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-026 all six allocation methods share one frozen basis

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-026 all six allocation methods share one frozen basis

expectedAssertions: exactly six stable method rows render
every row carries the same AllocationBasis fingerprint
method-specific assumptions and all feasible unstable infeasible or unavailable states remain visible

scenarios.title: No optimizer is the universal winner

gherkin.given: one allocation candidate has the strongest in-sample metric while others differ on drawdown cash-need survival turnover concentration and sensitivity
gherkin.then:
it states the tradeoffs by objective
And it does not label the strongest in-sample candidate best or recommended
And the user can inspect what assumption would reverse the apparent lead

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-allocation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-027 allocation comparison presents tradeoffs and no universal winner

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-027 allocation comparison presents tradeoffs and no universal winner

expectedAssertions: risk drawdown cash-need concentration turnover cost and stability tradeoffs are visible
presentation lens changes no candidate computation or global score
winner best recommended suitable apply and rebalance copy or controls are absent

scenarios.title: Estimation sensitivity exposes unstable weights

gherkin.given: a candidate is recomputed across declared history mean covariance and constraint perturbations
gherkin.then:
weight ranges turnover objective ranges and unstable holdings are visible
And the point-weight vector is labeled unstable when warranted by the declared policy
And no false precision is shown

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-allocation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-028 unstable allocation shows weight ranges and reversal conditions

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-028 unstable allocation shows weight ranges and reversal conditions

expectedAssertions: valid failed and infeasible trial counts are visible
per-asset weight turnover objective and path ranges plus reversal conditions are visible
unstable labels suppress false point precision

scenarios.title: Conflicting constraints return infeasible

gherkin.given: explicit minimums maximums exclusions liquidity and cash requirements conflict
gherkin.when: an allocation method attempts to solve
gherkin.then:
the candidate is infeasible
And the smallest identifiable conflicting constraint set is explained when possible
And no constraint is silently relaxed
And the current portfolio remains unchanged

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-allocation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-029 conflicting constraints remain infeasible without relaxation

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-029 conflicting constraints remain infeasible without relaxation

expectedAssertions: the candidate remains visibly INFEASIBLE
the deterministic irreducible conflict set and correction links are visible
all constraints and the current portfolio identity remain unchanged and no weight vector is applied

scenarios.title: Black-Litterman views are explicit not inferred

gherkin.given:
a behavior-derived interest exists for a market theme
And the user has not entered a Black-Litterman view for that theme
gherkin.then:
behavior history contributes no view return adjustment or confidence
And the candidate remains equilibrium-only or unavailable according to its explicit inputs
And any later user-entered view is labeled separately with sensitivity

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-allocation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-030 behavior cannot alter Black Litterman views returns or confidence

a confirmed user view remains separate from equilibrium posterior and sensitivity

scenarios.title: Walk-forward and costs limit backtest claims

gherkin.given: transaction costs rebalance timing walk-forward splits and trial count are available for an attractive historical rule
gherkin.then:
in-sample walk-forward and cost-adjusted results remain separate
And selection-bias and survivorship limitations are visible
And no historical result is described as proof of future superiority

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-allocation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-031 dossier separates in sample walk forward costs and trials

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-031 dossier separates in sample walk forward costs and trials

scenarios.title: Market-efficiency claims stay empirical

gherkin.given: the dossier identifies one efficiency form information set sample test and costs
gherkin.then:
it is limited to the tested weak semi-strong or strong-form proposition
And alternative explanations and data-snooping risk are visible
And the product does not state that all market-efficiency hypotheses are false

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-allocation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-032 efficiency claim is scoped to one tested information set

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-032 efficiency claim is scoped to one tested information set

scenarios.title: Correlation cannot adjudicate substantially identical

gherkin.given: two securities have very high historical correlation
gherkin.when: the user opens an analytical replacement comparison
gherkin.then:
correlation holdings overlap issuer or index facts and tracking evidence remain research inputs
And no numeric threshold labels the securities substantially identical or not substantially identical
And professional tax review is the explicit next boundary

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-allocation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-033 correlation never emits a substantially identical verdict

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-033 correlation never emits a substantially identical verdict

expectedAssertions: correlation overlap issuer index and tracking facts remain separate educational rows
no threshold yes no safe unsafe or filing instruction appears
facts-and-circumstances and professional tax review boundary are adjacent

scenarios.title: Recommendations remain non-executing

gherkin.given: fresh generic evidence and local exposure identify concentration and catalyst risk
gherkin.when: the Portfolio Brief authors an action
gherkin.then:
the action says review concentration inspect catalyst risk or run a scenario
And it includes condition invalidation or completion confidence freshness and deep link
And it contains no buy sell order trade size or automatic rebalance instruction

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-brief.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-034 every visible recommendation remains non executing research

trigger completion or invalidation confidence freshness and fixed deep link are visible

scenarios.title: Partial data does not create synthetic completeness

gherkin.given:
one holding has stale prices and another lacks factor history
And other holdings and generic evidence are current
gherkin.then:
valid current results remain visible
And stale-price and missing-factor impacts are named per result
And no missing value is treated as zero unchanged or average
And affected allocation or path confidence is reduced or unavailable by explicit policy

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-foundation.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-035 partial data corrupt schema and localStorage disabled preserve truth

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-035 partial data corrupt schema and localStorage disabled preserve truth

expectedAssertions: independently valid current results remain visible
stale missing quarantined corrupt and session-only reasons are attached to affected rows
no missing value becomes zero average unchanged or current and affected results reduce or withhold confidence

scenarios.title: One state feeds Simple and Power

gherkin.given: one portfolio revision evidence cutoff behavior state mandate and result identity are active
gherkin.when: the display mode or portfolio tab changes or a brief deep link is followed and returned
gherkin.then:
portfolio facts recommendations risk results paths candidate weights and caveats remain coherent
And display navigation causes no public publication or personal-data transmission
And Power adds evidence without upgrading or changing the conclusion

linkedTests: tests/portfolio-survival-mobile.spec.mjs :: Regression: SCN-008-036 Simple Power mobile and deep link return preserve one identity

linkedTestContracts.testId: Regression: SCN-008-036 Simple Power mobile and deep link return preserve one identity

expectedAssertions: all six tabs and both modes expose equal identity values states caveats and action ids
deep-link return restores focus and disclosure through session-only fixed-route handoff
